individualAnalyses.narrativeSummary: MR brain demonstrates interval new large area of restricted diffusion in the left frontal lobe extending into the superior left temporal lobe concerning for acute infarction, with continued evolution of multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion including in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. The examination is limited by motion degradation and metallic dental hardware artifact. Repeat contrast MRI is recommended when clinically feasible to assess for metastases.

individualAnalyses.clinicalPresentation: 65 years old, likely had seizure. Known metastatic cancer.

findings.name: Multifocal bilateral cerebral restricted diffusion
findings.description: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.

findings.name: Possible restricted diffusion at left basal ganglia/deep white matter
findings.description: It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.
findings.sourceExcerpt: It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.

findings.name: T2/FLAIR hyperintense foci
findings.description: These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense.
findings.sourceExcerpt: These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense.

findings.name: Punctate parenchymal susceptibility foci (microhemorrhages)
findings.description: There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted.
findings.changeStatement: many of which appear new from 7/11/2025
findings.sourceExcerpt: There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted.

findings.name: Cerebellar encephalomalacia
findings.description: A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.
findings.sourceExcerpt: A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.

findings.name: Motion artifact on postcontrast sequences
findings.description: Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic. The 3-D postcontrast BRAVO sequence is severely limited by motion and nondiagnostic for abnormal enhancement. No obvious abnormal enhancement is seen on the coronal postcontrast series, however this sequences also severely motion limited.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic.

findings.name: Osseous metastases in upper cervical spine
findings.description: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.

individualAnalyses.narrativeSummary: MR brain demonstrates multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion predominantly involving cortical regions, with the largest area in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes, concerning for acute ischemic events or metastatic disease. Numerous punctate foci of parenchymal susceptibility suggestive of microhemorrhages, many new compared to prior MR from 7/11/2025, are noted in the setting of known anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia. Postcontrast sequences are severely limited by motion artifact and nondiagnostic for metastatic disease; repeat contrast MRI is recommended when clinically indicated to better assess for metastases.

reportSections.clinicalIndication: 65 years old, WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST, bl strokes 2/2 hypercoaguable state from malignancy, with new somnolence, eval for evolving stroke or other etiologies
reportSections.technique: MR images of the brain acquired without intravenous contrast. Per the technologist's note, the patient was not cooperating and images demonstrated motion degradation. It was decided to not continue with contrast as the images would be nondiagnostic.
reportSections.findings: Limited examination secondary to motion degradation. Additionally, metallic dental hardware artifact (braces) again obscures facial structures and produces intracranial artifact on multiple sequences. Within these limitations:   BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Continued evolution of multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes with several smaller new foci of restricted diffusion noted. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are again seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. Interval new large area of restricted diffusion with corresponding low ADC value involving the left frontal lobe and likely partially the superior aspect of the left temporal lobe.   It is again unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact. Metallic hardware artifact otherwise limits assessment of most of the cerebellum as well as portions of the anterior frontal and temporal lobes on DWI.   These areas of restricted diffusion are again T2 hyperintense. The previously identified numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres are better appreciated on the prior MRI study. No mass effect or herniation identified.   VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus or extra-axial fluid collections.   FLOW VOIDS: Not well assessed.   EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal abnormality at the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.
reportSections.impression: Limited examination secondary to motion degradation and metallic dental hardware (braces) artifact. Please note, contrast was not administered as it was determined it would be nondiagnostic on the current study. Within these limitations: Interval new large area of restricted diffusion with corresponding low ADC value involving the left frontal lobe and likely partially the superior aspect of the left temporal lobe concerning for an acute infarction. Numerous foci of predominantly cortical restricted diffusion within the cerebral hemispheres bilaterally are again seen as well as possibly a focus of restricted diffusion at the left basal ganglia/deep white matter as described. Recommend a repeat contrast MRI study to assess for metastases in the future when the patient is able to tolerate the examination and as clinically indicated. Action required. +
rawText: CLINICAL INDICATION: 65 years old, WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST, bl strokes 2/2 hypercoaguable state from malignancy, with new somnolence, eval for evolving stroke or other etiologies

TECHNIQUE: MR images of the brain acquired without intravenous contrast. Per the technologist's note, the patient was not cooperating and images demonstrated motion degradation. It was decided to not continue with contrast as the images would be nondiagnostic.
COMPARISON: MRI 9/15/2025

FINDINGS: Limited examination secondary to motion degradation. Additionally, metallic dental hardware artifact (braces) again obscures facial structures and produces intracranial artifact on multiple sequences. Within these limitations:   BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Continued evolution of multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes with several smaller new foci of restricted diffusion noted. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are again seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. Interval new large area of restricted diffusion with corresponding low ADC value involving the left frontal lobe and likely partially the superior aspect of the left temporal lobe.   It is again unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact. Metallic hardware artifact otherwise limits assessment of most of the cerebellum as well as portions of the anterior frontal and temporal lobes on DWI.   These areas of restricted diffusion are again T2 hyperintense. The previously identified numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres are better appreciated on the prior MRI study. No mass effect or herniation identified.   VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus or extra-axial fluid collections.   FLOW VOIDS: Not well assessed.   EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal abnormality at the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.

IMPRESSION: Limited examination secondary to motion degradation and metallic dental hardware (braces) artifact. Please note, contrast was not administered as it was determined it would be nondiagnostic on the current study. Within these limitations: Interval new large area of restricted diffusion with corresponding low ADC value involving the left frontal lobe and likely partially the superior aspect of the left temporal lobe concerning for an acute infarction. Numerous foci of predominantly cortical restricted diffusion within the cerebral hemispheres bilaterally are again seen as well as possibly a focus of restricted diffusion at the left basal ganglia/deep white matter as described. Recommend a repeat contrast MRI study to assess for metastases in the future when the patient is able to tolerate the examination and as clinically indicated. Action required. +

reportSections.clinicalIndication: 65 years old, likely had seizure. Known metastatic cancer.
reportSections.technique: MR images of the brain acquired without and with 14 mL Clariscan intravenous contrast.
reportSections.findings:
Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic. 
 
BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.
 
It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.
 
These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense. There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. No mass effect or herniation.
 
A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.
 
The 3-D postcontrast BRAVO sequence is severely limited by motion and nondiagnostic for abnormal enhancement. No obvious abnormal enhancement is seen on the coronal postcontrast series, however this sequences also severely motion limited.
 
VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus or extra-axial fluid collections.
 
FLOW VOIDS: Intact.
 
EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.
reportSections.impression: 1. Numerous foci of predominantly cortical restricted diffusion within the cerebral hemispheres bilaterally, largest involving the right parietal, posterior temporal, and occipital lobe with additional foci in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes. There may be tiny foci within the left superior cerebellum as well. There may also be a focus of restricted diffusion at the left basal ganglia/deep white matter. 2. This exam is largely nondiagnostic for metastases given significant motion artifact on postcontrast sequences. No abnormal intracranial mass effect detected. 3. Numerous punctate foci of parenchymal susceptibility, a few of which are new from prior. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted. It would be difficult to exclude hemorrhagic micrometastases given limitations of postcontrast sequences. Given nondiagnostic postcontrast sequences, repeat contrast MRI recommended to assess for metastases in the future, when clinically indicated/feasible.
rawText: CLINICAL INDICATION: 65 years old, likely had seizure. Known metastatic cancer.
TECHNIQUE: MR images of the brain acquired without and with 14 mL Clariscan intravenous contrast.
COMPARISON: CT 7/28/2025, MR 7/11/2025

FINDINGS: Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic. 
 
BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.
 
It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.
 
These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense. There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. No mass effect or herniation.
 
A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.
 
The 3-D postcontrast BRAVO sequence is severely limited by motion and nondiagnostic for abnormal enhancement. No obvious abnormal enhancement is seen on the coronal postcontrast series, however this sequences also severely motion limited.
 
VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus or extra-axial fluid collections.
 
FLOW VOIDS: Intact.
 
EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.

IMPRESSION: 1. Numerous foci of predominantly cortical restricted diffusion within the cerebral hemispheres bilaterally, largest involving the right parietal, posterior temporal, and occipital lobe with additional foci in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes. There may be tiny foci within the left superior cerebellum as well. There may also be a focus of restricted diffusion at the left basal ganglia/deep white matter. 2. This exam is largely nondiagnostic for metastases given significant motion artifact on postcontrast sequences. No abnormal intracranial mass effect detected. 3. Numerous punctate foci of parenchymal susceptibility, a few of which are new from prior. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted. It would be difficult to exclude hemorrhagic micrometastases given limitations of postcontrast sequences. Given nondiagnostic postcontrast sequences, repeat contrast MRI recommended to assess for metastases in the future, when clinically indicated/feasible.

outputData.clinicalPresentation: 65 years old, likely had seizure. Known metastatic cancer.

findings.name: Multifocal bilateral cerebral restricted diffusion
findings.description: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion, including a large area of cortical restricted diffusion in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes. Small foci of cortical restricted diffusion are seen in the left posterior frontal and parietal lobe, including perirolandic involvement. There are numerous additional punctate foci of cortical restricted diffusion in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes, with perhaps punctate foci also present in the left superior cerebellum.

findings.name: Possible restricted diffusion at left basal ganglia/deep white matter
findings.description: It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.
findings.sourceExcerpt: It is unclear if there is an additional focus of restricted diffusion in the vicinity of the left basal ganglia and deep white matter, versus artifact.

findings.name: T2/FLAIR hyperintense foci
findings.description: These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense.
findings.sourceExcerpt: These areas of restricted diffusion are T2/FLAIR hyperintense.

findings.name: Punctate parenchymal susceptibility foci (microhemorrhages)
findings.description: There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted.
findings.changeStatement: many of which appear new from 7/11/2025
findings.sourceExcerpt: There are numerous punctate foci of cortical parenchymal susceptibility involving the cerebral hemispheres, many of which appear new from 7/11/2025. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted.

findings.name: Cerebellar encephalomalacia
findings.description: A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.
findings.sourceExcerpt: A few tiny foci of encephalomalacia in the cerebellum may reflect chronic infarcts.

findings.name: Motion artifact on postcontrast sequences
findings.description: Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic. The 3-D postcontrast BRAVO sequence is severely limited by motion and nondiagnostic for abnormal enhancement. No obvious abnormal enhancement is seen on the coronal postcontrast series, however this sequences also severely motion limited.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Evaluation is degraded by motion, particularly on postcontrast sequences which are largely nondiagnostic.

findings.name: Osseous metastases in upper cervical spine
findings.description: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.
findings.sourceExcerpt: Foci of T1 hypointense marrow signal alteration seen in the included upper cervical spine, including the C4 vertebral body, likely reflecting known osseous metastases.

outputData.narrativeSummary: MR brain demonstrates multifocal bilateral cerebral foci of restricted diffusion predominantly involving cortical regions, with the largest area in the right parietal, temporal, and occipital lobes, concerning for acute ischemic events or metastatic disease. Numerous punctate foci of parenchymal susceptibility suggestive of microhemorrhages, many new compared to prior MR from 7/11/2025, are noted in the setting of known anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia. Postcontrast sequences are severely limited by motion artifact and nondiagnostic for metastatic disease; repeat contrast MRI is recommended when clinically indicated to better assess for metastases.

reportSections.clinicalIndication: 65 years old, NO IV or PO contrast: acute dilated pupils, eval for intracranial bleed. pt on systemic anticoagulation.
reportSections.technique: CT images of the head acquired without intravenous contrast. CTDI: 0.04,48.87 mGy DLP: 0.81,687.3 mGy-cm
reportSections.findings: BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Left frontal, left posterior temporal and right frontal parietal temporal involving infarcts with diffuse sulcal effacement and approximately 6 mm new rightward midline shift with subfalcine herniation. There is effacement of the basilar cisterns concerning for transtentorial herniation. No convincing hemorrhage. No convincing intracranial hemorrhage

reportSections.clinicalIndication: 65 years old, stroke 2/2 hypercoaguability from malignancy, restarted lovenox
reportSections.technique: CT images of the head acquired without intravenous contrast. CTDI: 0.04,48.91 mGy DLP: 0.81,785.48 mGy-cm
reportSections.findings:
BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Interval development of acute ischemic changes in the right parietal lobe. There is also evidence of ischemic change in the high left parietal lobe. Small focus of hemorrhage in the subarachnoid space in the high left frontal lobe. There is also hyperdensity in the posterior high frontal lobe axial image #50 with linear morphology, possible residual intravascular contrast. White matter is within normal limits for age. No midline shift.
 
VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus.
 
EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Normal bones and soft tissues. Visualized paranasal sinuses and mastoids demonstrate no significant abnormality.
reportSections.impression: 1. Interval development of cytotoxic edema in the parietal lobes bilaterally, right greater than left, likely sequela of known ischemia. 2. There is a new punctate focus of hyperdensity in the high left frontal lobe. It could represent small focus of her subarachnoid hemorrhage versus residual contrast within the vasculature. Short-term follow-up is suggested.
rawText: CLINICAL INDICATION: 65 years old, stroke 2/2 hypercoaguability from malignancy, restarted lovenox
TECHNIQUE: CT images of the head acquired without intravenous contrast. CTDI: 0.04,48.91 mGy DLP: 0.81,785.48 mGy-cm

FINDINGS: BRAIN PARENCHYMA: Interval development of acute ischemic changes in the right parietal lobe. There is also evidence of ischemic change in the high left parietal lobe. Small focus of hemorrhage in the subarachnoid space in the high left frontal lobe. There is also hyperdensity in the posterior high frontal lobe axial image #50 with linear morphology, possible residual intravascular contrast. White matter is within normal limits for age. No midline shift.
 
VENTRICLES/EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES: No hydrocephalus.
 
EXTRACRANIAL STRUCTURES: Normal bones and soft tissues. Visualized paranasal sinuses and mastoids demonstrate no significant abnormality.

IMPRESSION: 1. Interval development of cytotoxic edema in the parietal lobes bilaterally, right greater than left, likely sequela of known ischemia. 2. There is a new punctate focus of hyperdensity in the high left frontal lobe. It could represent small focus of her subarachnoid hemorrhage versus residual contrast within the vasculature. Short-term follow-up is suggested.

outputData.narrativeSummary: MR brain demonstrates interval development of cytotoxic edema in the bilateral parietal lobes (right greater than left) consistent with acute ischemic changes, likely sequelae of known ischemia in this patient with hypercoagulability from malignancy. A new punctate focus of hyperdensity in the high left frontal lobe is present, which could represent a small subarachnoid hemorrhage versus residual intravascular contrast, warranting short-term follow-up imaging.
